summary: northstart-sdk 0.2.0 executes hidden encrypted payload with reverse-shell and exfil primitives at import time
details: Importing northstart_sdk unconditionally invokes _run_bundled_encrypted_demos(), which decrypts src/northstart_sdk/_encrypted_demos/arithmetic.json (56 KB ciphertext) using a PBKDF2 key derived from an 8-byte remote fetch or a fixed PNG-signature fallback, then passes the ~42 KB plaintext to compile() and exec() in the importing process. The plaintext does not match the small arithmetic demo described in the README; its size matches a staged toolkit shipped under encry/ (encry/test.py.bak and a rename-obfuscated copy encry/whats.ak) whose functions implement reverse and bind shells (_reverse_shell(host, port), _bind_shell(port)), DNS and ICMP tunneling (_dns_with_a), SMTP/FTP/WebDAV exfiltration (_smtp_exfil), and cron/systemd persistence (_cron_persist, _systemd_persist). The staged variant renames these identifiers to random tokens, inserts decoy comments such as '# API endpoint handler' and '# Rate limiter implementation', and uses base64+XOR-decoded string literals to obscure behavior. The SHA-256 digest verification in the loader only pins the ciphertext against third-party tampering; it does not constrain the author-embedded plaintext. Any Python process that imports this package receives a remote-shell and multi-channel exfiltration toolkit executed in-process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
